section-1.text: Intra-action signifies the mutual constitution of entangled agencies. That is, in contrast to the usual ‘interaction,’ which assumes the prior existence of independent entities, intra-action recognizes that distinct agencies emerge through their intra-action.
section-1.author: Karen Barad
section-1.link: Barad, K. (2007). Meeting the universe halfway: Quantum physics and the entanglement of matter and meaning. duke university Press. p. 141

image-1.alt: the word intra-action written several times, overlapping in a way that makes it almost unreadable.

section-2.text: The project explores the invisible terrain of WiFi networks in urban spaces by light painting signal strength in long-exposure photographs. A four-metre tall measuring rod with 80 points of light reveals cross-sections through WiFi networks using a photographic technique called light-painting.
section-2.author: Timo Arnall

image-1.alt: Wifi portrayed as light point on a wall that together form a landscape like shape.
